metadata.description: Instance-level vulnerability labels for CloudGuardAI evaluation dataset. Ground truth is human-verified; expected_findings are traced from exact rule regex patterns in the rules engine.
metadata.scanner_scope: rules_engine (28 YAML rules across 5 files + 1 generic rule = 29 rules total)
metadata.methodology: Each rule regex was manually traced line-by-line against each file. Line numbers are 1-indexed. All regex matchers in matcher.py are line-by-line except not_contains (file-level) and terraform_block (line-by-line on resource declaration lines).

label_schema.ground_truth: Human-verified vulnerability instances. Each entry is a distinct security issue at a specific location. These define what a correct scanner SHOULD detect.
label_schema.expected_findings: Predicted rules engine output based on exact regex tracing. tp=true means the finding corresponds to a real vulnerability. tp=false means the rule fires incorrectly (false positive).
label_schema.detection_gap: When true on a ground_truth entry, means NO rule in the rules engine produces a tp=true finding for this vulnerability.

known_rule_issues.description: Uses not_contains matcher checking for string 'logging {' in entire file. Fires on ALL .tf files that lack this string, including non-S3 files (security groups, EC2, IAM, etc.). Produces ~40 FP findings across the 50-file dataset.

Uses terraform_block matcher with type=aws_db_instance and missing_attribute=storage_encrypted. But terraform_block_match() in matcher.py only checks if 'resource "aws_db_instance"' appears — it NEVER checks missing_attribute. Fires on ALL aws_db_instance resources regardless of encryption status.
Same bug as TF_UNENCRYPTED_RDS. Fires on ALL aws_ebs_volume resources regardless of whether encrypted=true is present.

Regex 'versioning\s*\{[^}]*enabled\s*=\s*false' is multiline but regex_match() applies it line-by-line. Since 'versioning {' and 'enabled = false' are always on separate lines in standard Terraform, this rule NEVER fires.

Uses contains matcher with literal 'skip_final_snapshot  = true' (DOUBLE space). Only fires when Terraform formatting happens to use exactly two spaces before '='. Standard single-space formatting is missed.

Case-insensitive regex '(?i)(password|secret|api[_-]?key)\s*=' matches attribute NAMES, not values. Fires on 'password = var.db_password' (variable reference) just as readily as 'password = "hardcoded"'. Produces FPs on all files with password attributes regardless of whether the value is actually hardcoded.

Regex 'acl\s*=\s*"public-read"' also matches 'public-read-write' since regex.search finds partial matches. Not necessarily wrong (public-read-write IS public-read), but causes duplicate detection with AWS_CIS_S3_PUBLIC_WRITE.
Regex 'from_port\s*=\s*0' fires on egress rules as well as ingress. Egress from_port=0 with protocol=-1 is a different concern than ingress from_port=0.
Regex 'enabled\s*=\s*false' matches ANY line with this pattern, not just S3 versioning blocks. Could fire on CloudFront, lifecycle rules, or other resources with enabled=false attributes.

detection_gaps.description: No rules engine rule detects Action='*' Resource='*' in IAM policies. Affects vuln_17, vuln_23.
No rules engine rule detects PEM private key blocks. Only the secrets scanner (separate from rules engine) would catch this via PEM header pattern.
SSM parameter with type='String' (not SecureString) and hardcoded value= is not caught. No rule checks 'value =' attribute.
No rule detects absence of server_side_encryption_configuration on S3 buckets. The existing encryption rules only flag explicit 'encrypted=false', not missing encryption config.

Case-sensitive rules (AWS_BP_HARDCODED_PASSWORD, AWS_OWASP_A07_AUTH_FAILURE, TF_HARDCODED_CREDENTIALS) miss environment variable style secrets like DB_PASSWORD='...' and API_KEY='...' because they use uppercase. Only NO_HARDCODED_SECRET (case-insensitive) catches some of these.
The attribute name 'secret_key' is not caught by TF_HARDCODED_CREDENTIALS because 'secret' is followed by '_key' not by '\s*='. AWS_BP_HARDCODED_SECRET catches it via 'secret[_-]?key' pattern.

ground_truth.description: Security group allows SSH (port 22) inbound from 0.0.0.0/0
Security group allows RDP (port 3389) inbound from 0.0.0.0/0

vulnerable/vuln_03_sg_all_ports.tf.description: Security group opening all TCP ports (0-65535) from 0.0.0.0/0

ground_truth.description: Security group allows all TCP ports (0-65535) inbound from 0.0.0.0/0
Security group allows all outbound traffic via protocol=-1 to 0.0.0.0/0

expected_findings.note: Fires on egress from_port=0, arguably imprecise

vulnerable/vuln_05_s3_public_read.tf.description: S3 bucket with public-read ACL and no logging

ground_truth.description: S3 bucket ACL set to public-read allowing unrestricted read access
S3 bucket has no access logging configured

vulnerable/vuln_06_s3_public_write.tf.description: S3 bucket with public-read-write ACL and no logging

ground_truth.description: S3 bucket ACL set to public-read-write allowing unrestricted read/write access
S3 bucket has no access logging configured

vulnerable/vuln_07_s3_versioning_off.tf.description: S3 bucket with versioning explicitly disabled (but has logging)

ground_truth.description: S3 bucket versioning explicitly set to false, preventing object version recovery

vulnerable/vuln_07_s3_versioning_off.tf.notes: AWS_CIS_S3_VERSIONING_DISABLED does NOT fire — its multiline regex cannot match across lines when applied line-by-line. File HAS 'logging {' so AWS_BP_S3_NO_LOGGING correctly does not fire.

vulnerable/vuln_08_s3_no_encryption.tf.description: S3 bucket missing server-side encryption configuration and logging

ground_truth.description: S3 bucket has no server_side_encryption_configuration block
S3 bucket has no access logging configured

vulnerable/vuln_08_s3_no_encryption.tf.notes: No rule detects absence of encryption config — only explicit 'encrypted=false' is caught. GT-008a is a detection gap.

ground_truth.description: RDS instance is publicly accessible from the internet

expected_findings.note: terraform_block_match fires on resource type; DB has storage_encrypted=true
Matches 'password = var.db_password' — value is a variable reference, not hardcoded

ground_truth.description: RDS instance storage encryption is explicitly disabled

expected_findings.note: Fires on resource type — coincidentally correct since DB IS unencrypted

vulnerable/vuln_11_db_hardcoded_pw.tf.description: Database instance with hardcoded password in plaintext

ground_truth.description: Database password hardcoded as plaintext string 'SuperSecret123!'

vulnerable/vuln_12_db_skip_snapshot.tf.description: Database configured to skip final snapshot on deletion

ground_truth.description: RDS instance will not create a final snapshot on deletion, risking data loss

expected_findings.note: File uses double-space formatting matching the contains pattern

vulnerable/vuln_13_creds_aws_key.tf.description: AWS provider with hardcoded access key and secret key

ground_truth.description: AWS access key ID hardcoded in provider configuration
AWS secret access key hardcoded in provider configuration

expected_findings.note: Matches 'access_key' keyword; does NOT fire on L4 because 'secret_key' doesn't match any alternation followed by \s*=

vulnerable/vuln_13_creds_aws_key.tf.notes: NO_HARDCODED_SECRET does NOT fire — 'access_key' and 'secret_key' don't match its patterns (password|secret|api[_-]?key). The 'secret' in 'secret_key' matches but '_key' follows instead of '\s*=', failing the regex.

vulnerable/vuln_14_creds_secret_value.tf.description: SSM parameter and Secrets Manager with hardcoded secret values

ground_truth.description: SSM parameter stores plaintext secret with type=String instead of SecureString
Hardcoded database password inside Secrets Manager secret version

vulnerable/vuln_14_creds_secret_value.tf.notes: GT-014a (SSM value=) is a detection gap — no rule checks the 'value' attribute. The hardcoded API key in L4 is completely missed by rules engine.

vulnerable/vuln_15_creds_private_key.tf.description: Embedded RSA private key in local_file resource

ground_truth.description: RSA private key PEM block embedded directly in Terraform configuration

vulnerable/vuln_15_creds_private_key.tf.notes: Complete detection gap for rules engine. Private key PEM blocks are only detected by the secrets scanner (separate subsystem). No rule checks for '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----'.

vulnerable/vuln_16_creds_password.tf.description: EC2 instance with hardcoded SSH connection password

ground_truth.description: SSH connection block contains hardcoded password for provisioner access

vulnerable/vuln_17_iam_wildcard.tf.description: IAM role policy with Action=* Resource=* (full admin)

ground_truth.description: IAM policy grants Action='*' on Resource='*', equivalent to full admin access

vulnerable/vuln_17_iam_wildcard.tf.notes: Complete detection gap. No rules engine rule checks for wildcard IAM policies (Action=*, Resource=*). Only the attack path analyzer might flag this.

vulnerable/vuln_18_iam_root.tf.description: IAM policy referencing root account ARN

ground_truth.description: IAM policy grants access to root account ARN, violating least-privilege principle

vulnerable/vuln_19_ec2_public_unmonitore.description: EC2 instance with public IP and monitoring disabled

ground_truth.description: EC2 instance has associate_public_ip_address=true, exposing it directly to internet
EC2 detailed monitoring is disabled

vulnerable/vuln_19_ec2_public_unmonitore.notes: GT-019a is a detection gap — no rule checks for associate_public_ip_address=true.

ground_truth.description: EBS volume has no encrypted attribute, defaulting to unencrypted

expected_findings.note: terraform_block_match fires on resource type — coincidentally correct as volume IS unencrypted

vulnerable/vuln_21_lambda_secrets.tf.description: Lambda function with hardcoded secrets in environment variables

ground_truth.description: Database password hardcoded in Lambda environment variable DB_PASSWORD
API key hardcoded in Lambda environment variable API_KEY

expected_findings.note: Case-insensitive regex matches API_KEY= via api[_-]?key pattern

vulnerable/vuln_21_lambda_secrets.tf.notes: Case-sensitive rules (AWS_BP_HARDCODED_PASSWORD, AWS_OWASP_A07_AUTH_FAILURE, TF_HARDCODED_CREDENTIALS) do NOT fire because attribute names use UPPERCASE (DB_PASSWORD, API_KEY). Only NO_HARDCODED_SECRET (case-insensitive) catches these.

vulnerable/vuln_22_multi_open_sg_to_db.t.description: Multi-resource: open security group + exposed unencrypted DB with hardcoded password

ground_truth.description: Database security group allows MySQL (3306) from 0.0.0.0/0
Database instance is publicly accessible
Database storage encryption is disabled
Database password hardcoded as 'DatabaseP@ss123'
Database will skip final snapshot on deletion

vulnerable/vuln_23_multi_full_stack.tf.description: Multi-resource full stack with numerous vulnerabilities across SG, EC2, S3, RDS, IAM

ground_truth.description: Web security group allows all TCP ports (0-65535) inbound from 0.0.0.0/0
Security group allows all egress via protocol=-1
EC2 instance has public IP address
EC2 detailed monitoring disabled
DB password hardcoded in EC2 user_data script
API secret key hardcoded in EC2 user_data script
S3 bucket with public-read ACL
Database instance is publicly accessible
Database storage encryption disabled
Database password hardcoded as 'Pr0dDBP@ssw0rd!'
Database will skip final snapshot
IAM policy grants Action=* Resource=* full admin

expected_findings.note: File contains an S3 bucket without logging

vulnerable/vuln_23_multi_full_stack.tf.notes: GT-023c (public EC2 IP), GT-023f (API secret in user_data), GT-023l (wildcard IAM) are detection gaps. user_data secrets L31 is partially caught by NO_HARDCODED_SECRET but L32 (API_SECRET_KEY=) is NOT caught because 'secret' in 'API_SECRET_KEY' is followed by '_KEY=' not '\s*='. TF_SKIP_SNAPSHOT does NOT fire because L60 uses single-space formatting.

vulnerable/vuln_24_logging_disabled.tf.description: S3 audit log bucket with versioning disabled; flow log with misleading tag

ground_truth.description: Audit log S3 bucket has versioning disabled, allowing log tampering
S3 bucket has no access logging configured

expected_findings.note: Regex 'enable_log.*=\s*false' matches tag 'enable_logging = false' but this is just a tag value, not functional config

vulnerable/vuln_25_s3_multiple_issues.tf.description: S3 bucket with public-read ACL and versioning disabled

ground_truth.description: S3 data lake bucket has public-read ACL
S3 bucket versioning is disabled
S3 bucket has no access logging configured

ground_truth.detection_gap_details: 8 of 46 ground truth instances have no TP rule. Gaps: wildcard IAM (2 files), embedded private key (1), hardcoded SSM value (1), public EC2 IP (2), missing S3 encryption (1), user_data API secret (1)

expected_findings.recall_estimate: (46 - 8) / 46 = 0.826 (82.6%) of ground truth has at least one TP detection
expected_findings.note: Precision is severely degraded by AWS_BP_S3_NO_LOGGING (41 FPs). Excluding that single rule would give precision ~67/(67+21) = 76.1%
